summary: Formal specification for the Sovereign Organism Call Marketplace — a registry of 1176+ auto-generated callable tools produced by 12 Web Worker Builder AIs across 36 engines. 260 marketplace tools across 13 families (Context, Commander, Crawling, Sentry, Builder, Blueprint, Recipe, Lens, Hook, Trigger, Adapter, Sensor, Shield). 180 protocols. 60 architectural laws. Every call is registered, contracted, permissioned, priced, and law-enforced using phi-harmonic mathematics.

registrySchema.description: Schema for registering every callable tool and auto-generated call in the marketplace. Each call receives a unique call_id and is bound to exactly one worker and one engine.

call_id.description: Unique identifier for the call, auto-generated sequentially per worker prefix.

domain.description: Functional domain the call belongs to, aligned with Architectural Law domains.

worker_name.description: Latin name of the Web Worker Builder AI that owns this call.

engine_type.description: The engine class that executes this call within the worker.

target_module.description: The SDK module or internal module path the call targets.

version.description: Semantic version of the call definition.

created_at.description: Timestamp when the call was first registered.

toolMetadataSchema.description: Rich metadata attached to every tool and auto-generated call. Governs display, permissions, billing, trust, and law enforcement.

family.description: Functional family grouping for the tool.

purpose.description: Short description of what the tool does.

permission_class.description: Permission tier required to invoke this call.

house_placement.description: The architectural house/ring where this tool resides.

exposure_tier.description: Deployment exposure level determining who can discover and invoke the call.

latency_ms.description: Expected latency in milliseconds. Should ideally be a phi-derived value relative to HEARTBEAT_MS (873ms).

cost_weight.description: Relative cost weight for billing calculations. 0 = free, scaled by PHI per tier.

success_contract.description: Machine-readable assertion that must hold true for the call to be considered successful.

failure_contract.description: Machine-readable assertion describing expected failure shape.

sdk_dependencies.description: List of SDK packages required for this call.

laws_enforced.description: Architectural Laws (AL-001 through AL-040) that are enforced on this call.

callContractSchema.description: The contract between a caller and callee. Every call in the marketplace MUST define a contract specifying input/output shapes, timeout behavior, retry semantics, idempotency guarantees, side-effect declarations, and rollback capabilities.

input_schema.description: JSON Schema object defining the shape and validation rules for call inputs.

output_schema.description: JSON Schema object defining the shape of successful call output.

timeout_ms.description: Maximum time in milliseconds before the call is forcibly terminated. Defaults to PHI * HEARTBEAT_MS = 1412ms.

base_delay_ms.description: Base delay before first retry. Phi-exponential uses HEARTBEAT_MS * PHI_INV^attempt.

retry_on.description: Error codes that trigger a retry.

idempotent.description: Whether the call can be safely retried without side effects. Idempotent calls may be cached or deduplicated.

side_effects.description: Declared side effects of the call. Empty array means the call is pure/read-only.

rollback_capable.description: Whether the call supports automatic rollback on failure. Rollback-capable calls MUST implement a compensating transaction.

permissionTiers.description: Five hierarchical permission tiers governing call access. Each tier inherits all permissions from tiers below it. Escalation between tiers follows phi-timed delays and requires explicit authorization.

tiers.description: Internal-only calls accessible within the organism runtime. No external exposure. Used by core heartbeat, state management, and inter-worker communication.
Sovereign-grade calls with full organism authority. Governance, law enforcement, and architectural decisions. Accessible only to the organism owner and sovereign-tier workers.
Partner-tier calls available to trusted integration partners. Requires signed partner agreement and API key. Suitable for SDK consumers and whitelabel integrations.
tiers.accessScope: Registered partner organizations with signed agreements

tiers.description: Enterprise-tier calls for licensed enterprise deployments. Full API access with SLA guarantees. Requires enterprise license and contract verification via PROTO-006.
tiers.accessScope: Licensed enterprise customers with active contracts

tiers.description: Public-tier calls available to any authenticated user. Rate-limited, metered, and sandboxed. No state mutation permitted without explicit upgrade.
tiers.accessScope: Any authenticated user with valid API key

escalationRules.description: Escalation between permission tiers follows phi-timed delays. Each escalation level multiplies the base HEARTBEAT_MS by PHI^level.

pricingRewardClasses.description: Four pricing/reward classes governing how calls are billed and how workers are rewarded. Cost formulas use phi-weighted mathematics.

classes.description: No-cost calls for internal organism operations. Heartbeat, state sync, inter-worker messaging, and health checks. No billing, no metering.
Usage-tracked calls billed per invocation. Cost scales with phi-weighted cost_weight. Suitable for partner and public API calls.
Licensed calls requiring an active subscription or enterprise contract. Includes SLA guarantees, priority routing, and dedicated support.
Governance-grade calls for sovereign operations. Law enforcement, architectural changes, key rotation, and organism lifecycle management. Priced at phi-cubed weight. Requires sovereign authorization.

revenueDistribution.description: Revenue from metered and premium calls is distributed using phi-weighted splits.

rolloutTiers.description: Four-stage rollout pipeline for promoting calls from internal development to public availability. Each stage has minimum criteria that must be met before promotion.

stages.description: Initial deployment within the organism. Only accessible to internal workers and the sovereign. Used for development, testing, and validation.
Limited release to registered partners. Partner API keys required. Enables partner feedback, integration testing, and SLA validation before broader release.
Enterprise release with SLA guarantees and contract verification. Full audit logging, dedicated support, and compliance validation.
Full public availability. Rate-limited, metered, and sandboxed. Any authenticated user can discover and invoke the call. Maximum exposure with maximum safeguards.

rolloutFormulas.rollbackTrigger: failure_rate > max_failure_rate || uptime < min_uptime || trust_score < PHI_INV

workerRegistry.description: All 12 Web Worker Builder AIs with their Latin designations, domains, engine allocations, and call counts. Each worker operates 3 engines (Generator, Router, Builder) for a total of 36 engines across the organism.

Generator.purpose: Generate protocol schemas, consensus algorithms, and BFT configurations

Router.purpose: Route protocol messages, manage consensus rounds, and direct BFT voting

Builder.purpose: Build protocol implementations, assemble consensus modules, and deploy BFT nodes

Generator.purpose: Generate terminal interfaces, AI hierarchy schemas, and AGI tier definitions

Router.purpose: Route terminal commands, dispatch AI tasks, and manage AGI escalation

Builder.purpose: Build terminal components, assemble AI pipelines, and construct AGI workflows

Generator.purpose: Generate organism instances, SDK scaffolds, and emergence patterns

Router.purpose: Route organism signals, manage emergence events, and coordinate SDK lifecycles

Builder.purpose: Build organism runtimes, assemble SDK packages, and deploy emergence configurations

Generator.purpose: Generate tool schemas, marketplace listings, and tier configurations

Router.purpose: Route tool invocations, manage marketplace queries, and dispatch tier evaluations

Builder.purpose: Build marketplace components, assemble tool packages, and deploy tier policies

Generator.purpose: Generate house configurations, model manifests, and family registries

Router.purpose: Route model requests, dispatch to houses, and manage family selection

Builder.purpose: Build model pipelines, assemble house architectures, and deploy family configurations

Generator.purpose: Generate phi-weighted formulas, constant tables, and mathematical proofs

Router.purpose: Route computation requests, dispatch formula evaluations, and manage constant lookups

Builder.purpose: Build mathematical modules, assemble formula libraries, and deploy constant registries

Generator.purpose: Generate synapse configurations, chaos parameters, and connection topologies

Router.purpose: Route synapse signals, propagate chaos events, and manage connection graphs

Builder.purpose: Build synapse networks, assemble chaos engines, and deploy connection meshes

Generator.purpose: Generate blockchain schemas, node configurations, and layer definitions

Router.purpose: Route blockchain transactions, dispatch node operations, and manage layer routing

Builder.purpose: Build blockchain modules, assemble node clusters, and deploy layer stacks

Generator.purpose: Generate domain registries, ecosystem maps, and council constitutions

Router.purpose: Route domain queries, manage ecosystem events, and dispatch council decisions

Builder.purpose: Build domain infrastructure, assemble ecosystem platforms, and deploy council frameworks

Generator.purpose: Generate canister definitions, factory blueprints, and deployment manifests

Router.purpose: Route canister calls, dispatch factory operations, and manage canister lifecycle

Builder.purpose: Build canister modules, assemble factory pipelines, and deploy canister instances

Generator.purpose: Generate license templates, document schemas, and rights declarations

Router.purpose: Route license verifications, dispatch document processing, and manage rights checks

Builder.purpose: Build license enforcement modules, assemble document pipelines, and deploy rights management

Generator.purpose: Generate defense protocols, care policies, and arsenal inventories

Router.purpose: Route threat signals, dispatch care responses, and manage arsenal deployment

Builder.purpose: Build defense modules, assemble care frameworks, and deploy arsenal systems

engineTypes.description: Three engine types per worker, 36 engines total across 12 workers. Each engine type has a distinct role in the call lifecycle.

types.description: Produces new artifacts — schemas, configurations, templates, manifests, and code scaffolds. Generator engines are the creative force of each worker, responsible for auto-generating the calls themselves.
types.phiRole: PHI expansion — creates new structure from seed inputs

types.description: Routes, dispatches, and manages the flow of calls. Router engines are the nervous system of each worker, directing signals to the correct targets using phi-weighted routing algorithms.
types.phiRole: PHI_INV contraction — selects optimal path from many options

types.description: Assembles, deploys, and materializes artifacts into running systems. Builder engines transform generated schemas and routed instructions into deployed, operational components.
types.phiRole: PHI_SQUARED synthesis — combines generated and routed elements into whole systems

lawEnforcement.description: Every call in the marketplace is bound to one or more Architectural Laws (AL-001 through AL-040). Laws are enforced at invocation time via pre-call validation, during execution via runtime guards, and post-call via audit verification.

enforcementPhases.description: Before a call executes, all bound laws are checked. Permission tier, input schema, and trust level are validated against law requirements.
During execution, runtime guards enforce behavioral laws — unidirectional flow, heartbeat sovereignty, lineage preservation.
After execution, audit checks verify that success/failure contracts were honored and that no law was violated during execution.

lawToWorkerMapping.description: Each Architectural Law has primary affinity to specific workers based on domain alignment. A law may be enforced across multiple workers.

lawViolationSeverity.critical: Immediate call termination and sovereign alert
lawViolationSeverity.high: Call terminated, flagged for review within 1 heartbeat cycle
lawViolationSeverity.medium: Call allowed to complete, flagged for next audit cycle

callAllocationSummary.description: Summary of call allocation across all 12 workers, verifying the total of 776+ calls.
callAllocationSummary.phiDistributionNote: Call counts are distributed proportionally to domain complexity. ORGANISMUS (180) and PROTOCOLLUM (144) carry the largest loads as they form the organism core and protocol backbone. The ratio of the two largest workers (180/144 = 1.25) approximates PHI_INV squared (0.618^2 * PHI_SQUARED = 1.236).

sampleCalls.description: Representative sample calls demonstrating the full schema in action.

metadata.purpose: Generate JSON Schema definitions for new protocol specifications
Route consensus messages between BFT validator nodes with phi-weighted priority
Generate a new sovereign organism instance with 4-register architecture and heartbeat initialization
Generate marketplace tool schemas with full metadata, contracts, and law bindings
Generate phi-weighted mathematical formulas for cost, trust, latency, and reward calculations

versionHistory.description: Initial Call Marketplace Specification — 12 workers, 36 engines, 776 calls, 5 permission tiers, 4 pricing classes, 4 rollout stages, 40 laws enforced.
